reasoning: The Lean signature matches the informal statement exactly. G is an arbitrary group, H K are arbitrary subgroups of G, and `[Fintype H] [Fintype K]` captures the finiteness assumption. With `open Fintype`, `card H` refers to `Fintype.card H`, which is the order of the subgroup H as a group, so `Nat.Coprime (card H) (card K)` faithfully encodes "orders relatively prime." The conclusion `H ⊓ K = ⊥` is the standard Mathlib encoding of the intersection being the trivial subgroup {1}. No hypotheses are dropped or added, and the types of all objects align with the informal claim.
